title: Projection layer and Markdown content parser
description:
Build the pure projection layer that normalizes an Issue into the canonical JSON shape all rules validate against, plus the content-parser trait and its Markdown implementation.

## Background

JSON Schema rules need a stable, normalized view of an issue: labels grouped by namespace, attached doc types, and the description parsed into sections/items. This projection is the foundation every other engine component depends on, and it must be pure (in `domain/`) so it is testable without a filesystem. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine-plan.md` ("The Projection contract") and the decision record `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine.md` section 6.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] `domain/projection.rs` defines a `Projection` struct (`#[derive(Serialize, JsonSchema)]`) and a pure `project(&Issue, &dyn ContentParser) -> Projection` with no filesystem access.
- [ ] [hard] The projection groups labels by namespace (`labels.req = [...]`), exposes `type`/`state`/`priority`, `doc_types` (from `documents[].doc_type`), and `sections` parsed from the description.
- [ ] [hard] The projection JSON shape is documented as a stable contract; its schemars-generated schema is exposed and asserted by a test as the contract user schemas depend on.
- [ ] [hard] `document/parser/mod.rs` defines a `ContentParser` trait yielding one canonical structure (sections to items, headings, text), reusing `DocFormatAdapter::detect` for format detection rather than duplicating it.
- [ ] [hard] `document/parser/markdown.rs` implements the trait via pulldown-cmark (bump the stale 0.9 pin); a list item `- [hard] REQ-01: ...` projects deterministically into a `success_criteria` item.
- [ ] [hard] Unit tests cover label grouping, section/item extraction, and projection determinism.

## Notes

Anchors: `Issue` at `domain/types.rs:124-154`; `DocFormatAdapter` at `document/adapter.rs:24-51`; pulldown-cmark in `crates/jit/Cargo.toml`. Keep the layer pure per decision record section 12.
